headline: How Costco Cracked Japan’s Impossible Market
summary: Japan has long been a “graveyard” for foreign big box retailers — Walmart, Carrefour, and Tesco all failed to win over shoppers. Costco, on the other hand, has grown to nearly 40 locations since 1999, making Japan one of its largest markets outside North America.*Get more stories like this in your inbox for free:* 🔗 https://clickhubspot.com/0d83a1On paper, a bulk-buying warehouse shouldn’t have worked in one of the world’s most crowded countries. But Costco bent its US playbook to fit Japan’s unique retail landscape in surprising ways. This is the story of how Costco cracked one of the toughest markets in the world — and why its rivals couldn’t.Special thanks to Tran Phan for weighing in on our story. You can find more of his reporting here: https://www.readtrung.com/.And if you really haven’t had enough Costco content by this point, then check out our feature story by Zachary Crockett on how Costco gained a cult following — by breaking every rule of retail: https://thehustle.co/costco-membership-economics Subscribe to The Hustle:🔗 https://clickhubspot.com/vu3lGet the 5-minute newsletter keeping 2M+ innovators in the loop🔗 https://clickhubspot.com/3klnAbout HubSpot: HubSpot is a customer platform that provides education, software, and support to help businesses grow better. content.title: How Costco Cracked Japan’s Impossible Market
content.description:
Japan has long been a “graveyard” for foreign big box retailers — Walmart, Carrefour, and Tesco all failed to win over shoppers. Costco, on the other hand, has grown to nearly 40 locations since 1999, making Japan one of its largest markets outside North America.
*Get more stories like this in your inbox for free:* 🔗 https://clickhubspot.com/0d83a1

On paper, a bulk-buying warehouse shouldn’t have worked in one of the world’s most crowded countries. But Costco bent its US playbook to fit Japan’s unique retail landscape in surprising ways. This is the story of how Costco cracked one of the toughest markets in the world — and why its rivals couldn’t.

Special thanks to Tran Phan for weighing in on our story. You can find more of his reporting here: https://www.readtrung.com/.
And if you really haven’t had enough Costco content by this point, then check out our feature story by Zachary Crockett on how Costco gained a cult following — by breaking every rule of retail: https://thehustle.co/costco-membership-economics

headline: The Free Cash Flow Silence That Reshapes The Amazon Stock Bull Case
summary: Management's narrative has pivoted hard to AI and custom chips, but what they've stopped highlighting, the cash generation that funded it all, reveals a profound shift in the company's risk profile.

content.title: The Free Cash Flow Silence That Reshapes The Amazon Stock Bull Case
content.summary: Management's narrative has pivoted hard to AI and custom chips, but what they've stopped highlighting, the cash generation that funded it all, reveals a profound shift in the company's risk profile.

headline: Buy Walmart stock as price investment fear is "overdone", Bernstein says
summary: Investing.com -- In a note to clients on Tuesday, Bernstein reiterated an Outperform rating on Walmart with a price target of $145, arguing that investor concerns over the company's need for price investments following Kroger's announcement and related headlines are "overdone.”

content.title: Buy Walmart stock as price investment fear is "overdone", Bernstein says
content.summary: Investing.com -- In a note to clients on Tuesday, Bernstein reiterated an Outperform rating on Walmart with a price target of $145, arguing that investor concerns over the company's need for price investments following Kroger's announcement and related headlines are "overdone.”

headline: Jim Cramer: Dollar General Is a Hedge Fund Favorite as Rising Gas Prices Fuel Discount Retail
summary: Jim Cramer sees hedge funds quietly loading up on a familiar discount retailer as gas prices squeeze consumer budgets, and one recent earnings report suggests the trade may already be paying off.

content.title: Jim Cramer: Dollar General Is a Hedge Fund Favorite as Rising Gas Prices Fuel Discount Retail
content.summary: Jim Cramer sees hedge funds quietly loading up on a familiar discount retailer as gas prices squeeze consumer budgets, and one recent earnings report suggests the trade may already be paying off.

headline: Walmart (WMT) Stock Could Be 16% Overvalued Following Dividend Hike
summary: After a 159.9% total return over the past five years, Walmart now sits in an interesting spot for valuation focused investors, with recent checks pointing to a stock that screens rich on intrinsic value while looking roughly in line on earnings based multiples. Over five years, Walmart has returned 159.9%, which puts more pressure on today’s buyers to justify paying up for the stock at current levels. Walmart’s push into higher margin digital businesses, advertising and memberships can...

headline: Casey’s, Costco, and Other Consumer Stocks for Uncertain Times
